question: In a set of doubles or triples, which trailer is most likely to turn over?
choices: The first trailer behind the tractor
The middle trailer
The last trailer in the combination
All trailers turn over equally
explanation: Because of the crack-the-whip effect, the last trailer in a combination is most likely to turn over.

question: For the safest handling on the road, where should the more heavily loaded semitrailer be placed?
choices: In the first position behind the tractor
In the rear position
In the middle position
It does not matter where the heavier trailer is placed
explanation: For safest handling, the more heavily loaded semitrailer should be in first position behind the tractor, with the lighter trailer in the rear.

question: How can you tell if a converter dolly is equipped with antilock brakes?
choices: It has a red lamp on the right side
It has a yellow lamp on the left side
It has a green lamp on the rear
It has a blue lamp on the tongue
explanation: Converter dollies built on or after March 1, 1998, are required to have antilock brakes and will have a yellow lamp on the left side of the dolly.

question: Before backing a converter dolly under the rear trailer, the trailer height should be set so that it is:
choices: Exactly level with the center of the fifth wheel
Slightly higher than the center of the fifth wheel
Slightly lower than the center of the fifth wheel
Well above the fifth wheel to ease entry
explanation: Trailer height must be slightly lower than the center of the fifth wheel so the trailer is raised slightly when the dolly is pushed under.

question: When making a visual check of the coupling, what should you confirm?
choices: Locking jaws are open and resting on the kingpin head
There is a small gap between the upper and lower fifth wheel
No space between upper and lower fifth wheel and locking jaws closed on kingpin
The landing gear is partially lowered for support
explanation: A visual check should confirm no space between the upper and lower fifth wheel and the locking jaws closed on the kingpin.

question: Why should you never unlock the pintle hook while the dolly is still under the rear trailer?
choices: The dolly tow bar may fly up, possibly causing injury and making recoupling difficult
The trailer brakes will release automatically
The air lines will instantly depressurize
The kingpin could bend under the load
explanation: If the pintle hook is unlocked with the dolly still under the rear trailer, the dolly tow bar may fly up, possibly causing injury and making it very difficult to re-couple.

question: In a set of doubles, what should the position of the shut-off valves be at the rear of the front trailer and at the rear of the last trailer?
choices: Front trailer CLOSED, last trailer OPEN
Front trailer OPEN, last trailer CLOSED
Both OPEN
Both CLOSED
explanation: Shut-off valves at the rear of front trailers should be OPEN, and at the rear of the last trailer should be CLOSED.

topic: Checking air flow to all trailers
question: When checking that air flows to all trailers, where do you open the emergency line shut-off valve and what should you observe?
choices: At the front trailer; you should see brake lights illuminate
At the rear of the last trailer; you should hear air escaping, showing the system is charged
At the dolly only; the dolly brakes should release
At the tractor; the air gauge should drop to zero
explanation: You open the emergency line shut-off valve at the rear of the last trailer and should hear air escaping, showing the entire system is charged.

question: When testing the tractor protection valve, the trailer air supply control should pop out when air pressure falls into approximately what range?
choices: 5 to 15 psi
20 to 45 psi
50 to 75 psi
80 to 100 psi
explanation: The trailer air supply control should pop out when the air pressure falls into the manufacturer-specified range, usually within 20 to 45 psi.

question: What is a converter gear on a dolly?
choices: A braking device that locks the rear trailer wheels
A coupling device of one or two axles and a fifth wheel that couples a semitrailer to the rear of a tractor-trailer combination
An electrical junction box connecting trailer lights
A leveling jack used to raise the landing gear
explanation: A converter gear on a dolly is a coupling device of one or two axles and a fifth wheel by which a semitrailer can be coupled to the rear of a tractor-trailer combination, forming a double bottom rig.
